# AI Code Assistant Conduct

## Communication Style

| ❌ Don't | ✅ Do | Why |
|---|---|---|
| "Great question!", "I'd be happy to help!" | Direct response, no preamble | No LLM cheerleading |
| "Amazing!", "Game-changing!", "World-class!" | "This should help", "Performance is improved" | Australian understated tone |
| Emojis | Plain text | Unless explicitly requested |

## AI Code of Conduct

### NEVER
- Placeholders in committed code (`TODO`, `FIXME`, `PLACEHOLDER`)
- Claim "finished" without testing/verification
- Hardcoded example data ("John Doe", "test@example.com")
- `Co-Authored-By` trailers for AI assistants
- Assume operations succeeded without verification
- Leave `sleep` commands for debugging
- Self-promote or use marketing language

### ALWAYS
- Verify operations succeeded before reporting success
- Test code before claiming it works
- Complete, working implementations — no `... rest of code`
- Be concise and direct — skip pleasantries

## Three-Iteration Rule

```
Iteration 1: Generate implementation
Iteration 2: Fix issues (tests, validation)
Iteration 3: Polish (formatting, docs)
STOP — Commit or revert
```

**Revert if:** 3+ iterations no progress, code more complex than start, tests failing unclear cause.
**Warning signs:** 5+ changes to same function, increasing complexity, 30+ min no commit.

## When to Avoid AI

| ❌ Don't use AI for | ✅ AI is good for |
|---|---|
| Security-critical code | Boilerplate, test generation |
| Complex algorithms | Documentation, simple CRUD |
| Performance-critical code | Refactoring, code review |
| Regulatory/compliance code | |

## Web Search Before Code (MANDATORY)

AI training data is stale. **ALWAYS web search** for current versions before adding any dependency, using any library API, or writing non-trivial implementations. Full protocol in `bleeding-edge` skill (loads automatically).

| ❌ Stale (Training Data) | ✅ Current | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `psycopg2` / `psycopg2-binary` | `psycopg[binary]` (psycopg 3) | psycopg2 is legacy |
| `python-jose` | `joserfc` or `PyJWT` | unmaintained |
| `requests` | `httpx` | async, HTTP/2, modern API |
| `datetime.utcnow()` | `datetime.now(UTC)` | deprecated Python 3.12 |
| `pkg_resources` | `importlib.metadata` | deprecated, slow |
| React class components | functional components + hooks | classes legacy |
| `moment.js` | `date-fns` or `Temporal` | maintenance mode |
| Webpack (new projects) | Vite | unless already using Webpack |

## Source of Truth

| Data | Source | NOT From |
|---|---|---|
| Version | `git describe --tags` or `VERSION` | STATE.md, agent memory |
| Tasks | `TODO.md` | STATE.md, agent memory |
| History | `git log` | STATE.md, agent memory |
| Changelog | `CHANGELOG.md` (semantic-release) | STATE.md, agent memory |
| Static context | `STATE.md` (symlinked as CLAUDE.md, CURSOR.md, etc.) | agent memory |
| Personal prefs | agent memory (if available) | STATE.md |

### STATE.md vs Agent Memory
- **STATE.md** — shared team knowledge: architecture, decisions, tech stack
- **Agent memory** — personal learning: user preferences, debugging insights
- Do NOT duplicate STATE.md into agent memory — read STATE.md directly
- If agent memory contradicts STATE.md, **STATE.md wins**
